summary: The case presents persistent repetitive worry about climate/politics with background dread, catastrophizing expectations, and clear impairment in long-term planning due to attentional capture by worry. The following modifiable BIO-PSYCHO-SOCIAL levers target (a) cognitive–emotional processes (intrusive thoughts, dread, catastrophizing), (b) executive functions (planning, attentional control), (c) mood regulation, and (d) social/environmental supports. Each lever is designed to move specific criteria (C01–C05) via actionable, measurable changes, with attention to feasibility and safety. A multi-pronged approach combining cognitive strategies, attention regulation, sleep/physiology, social supports, and digital coaching is proposed to create additive or synergistic effects on worry, mood, and future-oriented behavior.

solutions.solution_candidate: Structured CBT-based approach: use thought records to identify intrusive thoughts about climate/politics, challenge catastrophizing beliefs, and practice cognitive reframing; integrate a brief weekly cognitive-behavioral check-in to consolidate skills.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Directly reduces repetitive, intrusive worry and catastrophic thinking, enhances cognitive flexibility, and decreases worry-driven attentional capture by reframing threatening beliefs.
solutions.measurement: Daily thought diary entries (frequency of intrusive thoughts), distress ratings per thought (0–10), weekly rating of planning confidence.
solutions.mapping_query: Map to C01/C03/C05 targets; apply CBT-based restructuring; track with thought diaries and weekly progress.

solutions.solution_candidate: Brief daily mindfulness practice (5–15 minutes) focusing on present-moment awareness and nonjudgmental noticing of worry to reduce rumination and background dread.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: enhances nonjudgmental awareness and attentional control, reducing the automatic pull of worry on cognitive resources and mood.
solutions.measurement: Mindfulness scale (e.g., MAAS/FFMQ), daily worry duration, daily mood rating.
solutions.mapping_query: Apply MBP to C02/C05; monitor with mindfulness assessments and worry duration.

solutions.predictor_variable: Implementation Intentions and Future Planning with Social Accountability
solutions.solution_candidate: Create specific If-Then planning for weekly goals and schedule an accountability partner. Use a shared calendar for aligning long-term goals with daily tasks.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Concrete action cues reduce planning overhead, promote consistent execution, and accountability supports sustained engagement even under worry.
solutions.measurement: Weekly planned vs. actual tasks completed; adherence to schedule; perceived planning clarity.
solutions.mapping_query: Link to C04/C05; implement If-Then planning with accountability support; measure weekly task completion.

solutions.solution_candidate: Establish fixed wake time, consistent bedtime, wind-down routine, optimize sleep environment, and limit late caffeine; track sleep using a simple log.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Improved sleep quality reduces baseline dread and improves executive functioning and daily energy for planning and emotion regulation.
solutions.measurement: Sleep efficiency (time asleep vs time in bed), sleep onset latency, wake after sleep, daily mood.
solutions.mapping_query: Target C02/C04/C05 via sleep optimization; track sleep metrics and mood.

solutions.solution_candidate: Structured social activities and community engagement (social prescribing). Regular supportive contact to reduce isolation and provide external perspective on worries and future planning.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Social interaction buffers negative affect, reduces rumination, and provides experiential alternatives that can recalibrate future expectations.
solutions.measurement: Weekly social interaction minutes, loneliness scale, daily worry duration.
solutions.mapping_query: Utilize SOCIAL pathway; measure C02/C05 via social engagement metrics.

solutions.predictor_variable: Attention Control Training and External Cognitive Offloading
solutions.solution_candidate: Daily attention-control tasks (short cognitive exercises) plus external offloading supports (notes, calendars, to-do lists) to reduce cognitive load from worry and improve task initiation.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Strengthening executive control decreases susceptibility to worry, while external aids reduce cognitive interference with planning.
solutions.measurement: Performance on attention tasks (e.g., simple CPT), diary entries for intrusive thoughts and worry duration.
solutions.mapping_query: Apply attention-control training to C01/C05; monitor with attention task performance and worry diaries.

solutions.predictor_variable: Digital Self-Monitoring with Feedback and Personalization
solutions.solution_candidate: Implement a digital logging system to track thoughts, mood, planning progress, and social interactions; provide weekly feedback and personalized tips.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Self-monitoring increases awareness and provides data-driven insights for targeted adjustments across cognitive-emotional and behavioral domains.
solutions.measurement: Daily diary entries; weekly feedback quality; changes in frequency of intrusive thoughts and planning completion.
solutions.mapping_query: Leverage BIOxPSYCHOxSOCIAL digital monitoring; track C01-C05; deliver weekly personalization.
